memory.summary: I see a chance to relax now, but the looming project deadline makes me hesitate to prioritize leisure over future security.
Spending thirty minutes on a trivial chore felt like an instant win, but it barely moved the needle on my larger project worries.
That quick chore win felt good, but it just highlighted how far behind the main project still is as the afternoon slips away.
That quick win felt satisfying, yet it only deepens my worry that I'm trading real progress for easy relief while the deadline looms.

I traded energy for a hollow win, letting the real deadline slip while my stress quietly climbs.

I traded my already depleted energy for a hollow win, letting the real deadline slip while stress quietly climbs.
This tiny win feels like a band-aid on a wound, leaving my energy drained while the real deadline I care about slips further away.

desires: Stop the hollow feeling of wasting my only remaining energy on trivial wins while the term project deadline looms
Protect my reputation with cohort_3 by not appearing unreliable or scattered before they notice my avoidance
Regain enough mental clarity to actually start the future_project without letting stress spiral further

goals.description: Complete the trivial chore immediately to remove the 'unfinished business' hanging over my head, allowing me to be fully present with cohort_3 tonight without feeling like a slacker.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) and avoidant conflict style make the tension of an incomplete task unbearable right before social time. Even though I've only made small progress (0.56), the psychological friction of leaving it half-done will bleed into our meal, risking the high trust (6) and closeness (7) I have with them. Finishing this now is the only way to protect our relationship dynamic.

goals.description: Eat a relaxed meal with cohort_3, focusing entirely on connection rather than productivity, to recharge my emotional battery.
goals.rationale: With relationships weighted highest in my values and my current stress at a moderate level (4), prioritizing this meal is non-negotiable. My deliberative nature means I know that if I show up distracted or apologetic about unfinished work, it creates unnecessary interpersonal friction. This long-term investment in my support network is more critical than solving the term project panic right now.

agent.last_reasoning: Finishing the chore now removes the mental block before I meet cohort_3 tonight. My affiliation value (0.5) demands I show up fully present; lingering guilt would strain our high trust bond. This short burst protects the meal's quality without derailing my long-term study horizon.

goals.rationale: As an affiliation-driven individual, I need to enter social interactions feeling light and capable. This low-effort task is a quick win that preserves my energy for the more important goal of maintaining harmony in cohort_3 without risking burnout.

goals.description: Draft a brief, non-confrontational update to the team about project progress (focusing on 'we are moving forward') rather than addressing the professor's specific demands directly right now.
goals.rationale: My avoidant conflict style makes me hesitant to push back on authority figures like the professor. Addressing this internally or later avoids immediate friction, while a vague but reassuring message to the team satisfies my high priority on relationship stability.

desires: Maintain my standing as a reliable teammate within cohort_3 by ensuring the term project doesn't slip due to my hesitation.

Avoid the immediate friction of pushing back on the professor's expectations, even if it means carrying extra stress internally.
